The hierarchical partition layout of dual-path wiring is adopted, and the main and standby lines are interconnected with ground isolation and the SI characteristics are verified separately
B
All control signals are transmitted using differential pairs and traced to the center area to avoid path inconsistencies
C
Use the shortest path and add a dynamic switching module at the intersection node to improve signal transfer efficiency
D
The main signal is widened for enhanced electrical stability, and the backup path is simplified with a single-layer redundant connection
E
None of the above options are correct

question: How to reduce package return loss when routing from die to external interface?

All I/O signals are concentrated to the edge traces of the package to shorten the path without focusing on the impedance hop position
B
Use redundant packaged channels to increase signal flux and improve reflected wave absorption efficiency
C
Impedance matching of the core chip I/O region, using short fan-out paths and matching terminal structures to reduce reflection
D
The interface signal is uniformly transferred to the high-speed buffer and then connected to the outside to stabilize the level jitter

question: How to mitigate signal integrity degradation under flexing in flex-rigid boards?

All key signals should be routed differentially and centrally arranged in the rigid area to avoid deformation in the flexible area

Use serpentine buffer traces in the flexible area, avoid right angles in the turning area, and add mechanical buffer to the connection port

Use high-speed copper foil to match with stiffeners to introduce impedance compensation in the bending area to improve signal integrity

Large-area pads and high-density through-hole structure are used to improve structural strength and avoid the risk of low-temperature fracture

All optical channels are arranged in a centralized manner for uniform focus, calibration, and compression of interconnection bandwidth allocation

Uses single-path fiber and enhances heat dissipation to extend laser life without considering signal interference issues

The optical interconnection should keep the optical path straight, isolate the signal line interference in the electro-optical conversion area, and reserve the heat dissipation path to prevent laser drift

All signal lines can be concentrated on the top layer of wiring to uniformly add shielding to reduce stray interference and leakage

The signal is traced to the ground, avoiding long stubs, avoiding the trace passing through the cutting area of the reference layer, and adding a common-mode filter structure if necessary

The dimming channel is prioritized to the inner layer and an additional clock calibration module is introduced synchronously to improve the synchronization ability

All PWM lines uniformly bypass the periphery of the main control chip and enter the output port after being buffered by the inverter
D
PWM wire equal length wiring and ground protection wire, drive area plus buffer circuit to avoid signal distribution path spanning multiple power supply areas
E
None of the above options are correct

question: How to avoid resource conflicts between on-chip buses and peripheral access in embedded AI accelerators?
options: A
Peripheral access adopts bridge multiplexing, unifies the entrance shared bus structure, and increases scheduling sequence management to improve efficiency
B
Hierarchical wiring of internal and external buses, peripheral paths plus multi-channel arbitrators, and key bus areas with equal length priority channel guidance
C
Route all bus access paths to the central switching node and schedule peripheral requests through time slices
D
On-chip resources are allocated to peripheral interfaces first, and the main channel adopts serial scheduling to reduce path occupancy
E
None of the above options are correct

question: How to avoid shared bus bottlenecks impacting high-throughput paths in RISC-V multicore AI chip routing?
options: A
Multi-core access is mapped to the same data path, relying on the caching system to dynamically adjust the communication rate to avoid blocking
B
The high-speed core is prioritized for access to the main bus, and the low-speed channel adopts a ring structure scheduling and adds a priority control mechanism
C
The high-throughput path uses a dedicated direct connection channel, and the on-chip bus is layered and controlled by an arbiter to maintain the equivalent delay of the access path
D
The inter-core bus is equipped with an asynchronous cross-interconnection structure, and the global buffer is used to control the transmission direction of burst traffic
E
None of the above options are correct

question: How to reduce vertical interconnect loss for high-speed signals in Chiplet-based PoP packaging?
options: A
Increase the interconnection bandwidth by increasing the number of vertical layers of PoP, supplemented by the global power network to control signal jump jitter
B
High-speed signals are routed using symmetrical TSV or μBump wiring structures, with buffers inserted into the transition zone and placed close to the reference layer
C
All vertical interconnection signals are centrally routed to the packaging center area, and then centralized impedance matching is carried out uniformly
D
Multi-stage capacitive isolation and reflective absorption layer design are used to guide high-speed signal transmission along the thermally coupled path to avoid interference
E
None of the above options are correct
